description: You use the elements in the math folder to perform mathematical operations on string-based numeric values.

services.name: pub.math:absoluteValue
description: WmPublic. Returns the absolute value of the input number.

input_parameters.name: num
description: Number whose absolute value is to be returned.

output_parameters.name: positiveNumber
description: Absolute value of the input number.

services.name: pub.math:addFloatList
description: WmPublic. Adds a list of floating point numbers (represented in a string list) and returns the sum.

input_parameters.name: numList
description: Numbers (floating point numbers represented in a string list) to add.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Sum of the numbers in numList. If a sum cannot be produced, value contains one of the following:

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in numList are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:addFloats
description: WmPublic. Adds one floating point number (represented as a String) to another and returns the sum.

input_parameters.name: num1
description: Number to add.

input_parameters.name: num2
description: Number to add.

input_parameters.name: precision
description: The precision parameter, if specified, will override the behavior set by the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property. For information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see the IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

output_parameters.name: value
description: Sum of the numbers in num1 and num2. If a sum cannot be produced, value contains one of the following:

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

Use the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property to specify whether the pub.math:addFloats service return the exact result of an operation involving two floating point numbers, the result as calculated by the JVM, or the result based on a fixed number of decimal places. For information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see the IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

services.name: pub.math:addIntList
description: WmPublic. Adds a list of integers (represented in a String list) and returns the sum.

input_parameters.name: numList
description: Numbers (integers represented as Strings) to add.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Sum of the numbers in numList.

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in numList are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:addInts
description: WmPublic. Adds one integer (represented as a String) to another and returns the sum.

input_parameters.name: num1
description: Number (integer represented as a String) to add.

input_parameters.name: num2
description: Number (integer represented as a String) to add.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Sum of num1 and num2.

usage_notes: Make sure the result of your calculation is less than 64 bits in width (the maximum width for the long data type). If the result exceeds this limit, it will generate a data overflow.

Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:addObjects
description: WmPublic. Adds one java.lang.Number object to another and returns the sum.

input_parameters.name: num1
description: Number to add. See the Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

input_parameters.name: num2
description: Number to add. See the Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Sum of the numeric values of num1 and num2.

usage_notes: This service accepts the following sub-classes of java.lang.Number: java.lang.Byte, java.lang.Double, java.lang.Float, java.lang.Integer, java.lang.Long, java.lang.Short.
This service applies the following rules for binary numeric promotion to the operands in order:
- If either operand is of type Double, the other is converted to Double.
- Otherwise, if either operand is of type Float, the other is converted to Float.
- Otherwise, if either operand is of type Long, the other is converted to Long.
- Otherwise, both operands are converted to type Integer.
These promotion rules mirror the Java rules for numeric promotion of numeric types.

services.name: pub.math:divideFloats
description: WmPublic. Divides one floating point number (represented as a String) by another (num1/num2) and returns the quotient.

input_parameters.name: num1
description: Number (floating point number represented as a String) that is the dividend.

input_parameters.name: num2
description: Number (floating point number represented as a String) that is the divisor.

input_parameters.name: precision
description: The precision parameter, if specified, will override the behavior set by the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property. For information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see the IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

output_parameters.name: value
description: The quotient of num1 / num2. If a quotient cannot be produced, value contains one of the following:

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

Use the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property to specify whether the pub.math:divideFloats service return the exact result of an operation involving two floating point numbers, the result as calculated by the JVM, or the result based on a fixed number of decimal places. For information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see the IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

services.name: pub.math:divideInts
description: WmPublic. Divides one integer (represented as a String) by another (num1/num2) and returns the quotient.

input_parameters.name: num1
description: Number (integer represented as a String) that is the dividend.

input_parameters.name: num2
description: Number (integer represented as a String) that is the divisor.

output_parameters.name: value
description: The quotient of num1 / num2.

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:divideObjects
description: WmPublic. Divides one java.lang.Number object by another (num1/num2) and returns the quotient.

input_parameters.name: num1
description: Number that is the dividend. See the Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

input_parameters.name: num2
description: Number that is the divisor. See the Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Quotient of num1 / num2.

usage_notes: This service accepts the following sub-classes of java.lang.Number: java.lang.Byte, java.lang.Double, java.lang.Float, java.lang.Integer, java.lang.Long, java.lang.Short.
This service applies the following rules for binary numeric promotion to the operands in order:
- If either operand is of type Double, the other is converted to Double.
- Otherwise, if either operand is of type Float, the other is converted to Float.
- Otherwise, if either operand is of type Long, the other is converted to Long.
- Otherwise, both operands are converted to type Integer.
These promotion rules mirror the Java rules for numeric promotion of numeric types.

services.name: pub.math:max
description: WmPublic. Returns the largest number from a list of numbers.

input_parameters.name: numList
description: List of numbers from which the largest number is to be returned.

output_parameters.name: maxValue
description: Largest number from the list of numbers.

services.name: pub.math:min
description: WmPublic. Returns smallest number from a list of numbers.

input_parameters.name: numList
description: List of numbers from which the smallest number is to be returned.

output_parameters.name: minValue
description: Smallest number from the list of numbers.

services.name: pub.math:multiplyFloatList
description: WmPublic. Multiplies a list of floating point numbers (represented in a String list) and returns the product.

input_parameters.name: numList
description: Numbers (floating point numbers represented as Strings) to multiply.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Product of the numbers in numlist. If a product cannot be produced, value contains one of the following:

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in numList are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:multiplyFloats
description: WmPublic. Multiples one floating point number (represented as String) by another and returns the product.

input_parameters.name: num1
description: Number (floating point number represented as a String) to multiply.

input_parameters.name: num2
description: Number (floating point number represented as a String) to multiply.

input_parameters.name: precision
description: The precision parameter, if specified, will override the behavior set by the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property. For information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see the IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

output_parameters.name: value
description: Product of the numeric values of num1 and num2. If a product cannot be produced, value contains one of the following:

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

Use the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property to specify whether the pub.math:multiplyFloats service return the exact result of an operation involving two floating point numbers, the result as calculated by the JVM, or the result based on a fixed number of decimal places. See IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide for more information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property.

services.name: pub.math:multiplyIntList
description: WmPublic. Multiplies a list of integers (represented in a String list) and returns the product.

input_parameters.name: numList
description: Numbers (floating point numbers represented as Strings) to multiply.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Product of the numbers in numList.

usage_notes: Make sure the result of your calculation is less than 64 bits in width (the maximum width for the long data type). If the result exceeds this limit, it will generate a data overflow.

Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in numList are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:multiplyInts
description: WmPublic. Multiplies one integer (represented as a String) by another and returns the product.

input_parameters.name: num1
description: Number (integer represented as a String) to multiply.

input_parameters.name: num2
description: Number (integer represented as a String) to multiply.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Product of num1 and num2.

usage_notes: Make sure the result of your calculation is less than 64 bits in width (the maximum width for the long data type). If the result exceeds this limit, it will generate a data overflow.

Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:multiplyObjects
description: WmPublic. Multiplies one java.lang.Number object by another and returns the product.

input_parameters.name: num1
description: Number to multiply. See the Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

input_parameters.name: num2
description: Number to multiply. See the Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Product of num1 and num2.

usage_notes: This service accepts the following sub-classes of java.lang.Number: java.lang.Byte, java.lang.Double, java.lang.Float, java.lang.Integer, java.lang.Long, java.lang.Short.
This service applies the following rules for binary numeric promotion to the operands in order:
- If either operand is of type Double, the other is converted to Double.
- Otherwise, if either operand is of type Float, the other is converted to Float.
- Otherwise, if either operand is of type Long, the other is converted to Long.
- Otherwise, both operands are converted to type Integer.
These promotion rules mirror the Java rules for numeric promotion of numeric types.

services.name: pub.math:randomDouble
description: WmPublic. Returns the next pseudorandom, uniformly distributed double between 0.0 and 1.0.
Random number generators are often referred to as pseudorandom number generators because the numbers produced tend to repeat themselves over time.

output_parameters.name: number
description: Generated random number.

services.name: pub.math:roundNumber
description: WmPublic. Returns a rounded number.

input_parameters.name: num
description: Number to be rounded.

input_parameters.name: numberOfDigits
description: Specifies the number of digits to which you want to round the number.

input_parameters.name: roundingMode
description: Valid values for the roundingMode parameter are RoundHalfUp, RoundUp, RoundDown, RoundCeiling, RoundFloor, RoundHalfDown, and RoundHalfEven. The default value is RoundHalfUp.

output_parameters.name: roundedNumber
description: The rounded number.

services.name: pub.math:subtractFloats
description: WmPublic. Subtracts one floating point number (represented as a String) from another and returns the difference.

input_parameters.name: num1
description: Number (floating point number represented as a String).

input_parameters.name: num2
description: Number (floating point number represented as a String) to subtract from num1.

input_parameters.name: precision
description: The precision parameter, if specified, will override the behavior set by the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property. For information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see the IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

output_parameters.name: value
description: Difference of num1 - num2. If a difference cannot be produced, value contains one of the following:

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

Use the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property to specify whether the pub.math:subtractFloats service return the exact result of an operation involving two floating point numbers, the result as calculated by the JVM, or the result based on a fixed number of decimal places. For more information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide

services.name: pub.math:subtractInts
description: WmPublic. Subtracts one integer (represented as a String) from another and returns the difference.

input_parameters.name: num1
description: Number (integer represented as a String).

input_parameters.name: num2
description: Number (integer represented as a String) to subtract from num1.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Difference of num1 - num2.

usage_notes: Make sure the result of your calculation is less than 64 bits in width (the maximum width for the long data type). If the result exceeds this limit, it will generate a data overflow.

Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1 andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:subtractObjects
description: WmPublic. Subtracts one java.lang.Number object from another and returns the difference.

input_parameters.name: num1
description: Number. See the Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

input_parameters.name: num2
description: Number to subtract from num1. See Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Difference of num1 - num2.

usage_notes: This service accepts the following sub-classes of java.lang.Number: java.lang.Byte, java.lang.Double, java.lang.Float, java.lang.Integer, java.lang.Long, java.lang.Short.
This service applies the following rules for binary numeric promotion to the operands. The following rules are applied in order:
- If either operand is of type Double, the other is converted to Double.
- Otherwise, if either operand is of type Float, the other is converted to Float.
- Otherwise, if either operand is of type Long, the other is converted to Long.
- Otherwise, both operands are converted to type Integer.
These promotion rules mirror the Java rules for numeric promotion of numeric types.

services.name: pub.math:toNumber
description: WmPublic. Converts a string to numeric data type.

input_parameters.name: num
description: Number (represented as a string) to be converted to numeric format.

input_parameters.name: convertAs
description: Valid values for the convertAs parameter are java.lang.Double, java.lang.Float, java.lang.Integer,java.math.BigDecimal,java.math.BigInteger, java.lang.Long. The default value is java.lang.Double.

output_parameters.name: num
description: Converted numeric object.
